Metadata.Tribunal_Court: General Division of the High Court of the Republic of Singapore

Party_Details.Name: Tendcare Medical Group Holdings Pte Ltd

Facts.Text: Tendcare, then known as Tian Jian Hua Xia Medical Group Holdings Pte Ltd, was incorporated in Singapore by Mr Gong on 20 February 2014
HXG entered into a memorandum of understanding with BJTJ for the purpose of an IPO of Tendcare
Tendcare received US$14,992,805.48 and US$5,007,187.57 from EFG Atlantis and Atlantis China respectively
Tendcare received US$49,987,532.13 from Chow Tai Fook Nominees Ltd in respect of the Easom investment
OCA agreed to provide a US$19,978,280 loan to Tendcare via a Convertible Note Subscription Agreement

Party_Details.Issue: Whether the business of Tendcare was carried on with intent to defraud creditors or for a fraudulent purpose
Party_Details.Rule: A business is carried on with intent to defraud if there is dishonesty in the form of deception practised on an innocent party
Party_Details.Application: The defendants engaged in fraudulent trading by using the Tendcare IPO as a deliberate ruse to raise funds which were subsequently misappropriated.
Party_Details.Judicial_Reasoning_Log: The court found that the pre-IPO funds were misappropriated and the Tendcare IPO was never intended to proceed.

Metadata.Tribunal_Court: General Division of the High Court of the Republic of Singapore

Party_Details.Name: Gong Ruizhong

Facts.Text: Mr Gong was appointed director of Tendcare and held the position at all material times
Tendcare transferred US$5m to HXTJ shortly after receiving funds from EFG Atlantis and Atlantis China
On 6 February 2015, Tendcare transferred US$15m to HXTJ after receiving funds from Chow Tai Fook Nominees Ltd
Tendcare transferred US$4m to Tian Jian HK, which was then transferred to QHC
Tendcare transferred US$3m to HXTJ after receiving funds from Chow Tai Fook Nominees Ltd

Party_Details.Issue: Whether Mr Gong was knowingly a party to the carrying on of Tendcare's business with intent to defraud
Party_Details.Rule: A person is knowingly a party to fraudulent trading if they authorize or cause transactions that misappropriate funds
Party_Details.Application: The fund transfers were executed for legitimate business purposes and there was no intent to defraud creditors or misappropriate funds.
Party_Details.Judicial_Reasoning_Log: The court found that Mr Gong was the person behind the Scheme and liable for fraudulent trading.

Metadata.Tribunal_Court: General Division of the High Court of the Republic of Singapore

Party_Details.Name: Hua Xia Tian Jian Pte Ltd

Facts.Text: Entrustment Agreement between HXTJ and Tendcare was dated 2014-03-03
On 2015-02-06, Tendcare transferred US$15m to HXTJ, and on 2015-02-09, it transferred US$5m to HXTJ

Party_Details.Issue: Whether HXTJ is entitled to reimbursement for sums disbursed on behalf of Tendcare under the Entrustment Agreement
Party_Details.Rule: A party is entitled to reimbursement for expenses incurred on behalf of another if those expenses were authorized under a valid and binding agreement
Party_Details.Application: The company is legally entitled to reimbursement for sums validly disbursed on behalf of Tendcare under the binding Entrustment Agreement.
Party_Details.Judicial_Reasoning_Log: The court found no satisfactory evidence of the Entrustment Agreement's authenticity or the proper use of funds.

Metadata.Tribunal_Court: General Division of the High Court of the Republic of Singapore

Party_Details.Name: Tendcare Medical Group Holdings Pte Ltd

Facts.Text: On 2015-02-06, Tendcare transferred US$15m to HXTJ

Party_Details.Issue: Whether Tendcare is legally responsible to reimburse HXTJ for sums allegedly disbursed under the Entrustment Agreement
Party_Details.Rule: A company is legally responsible for reimbursement if it is proven that funds were disbursed under a valid agreement for legitimate purposes
Party_Details.Application: The purported Entrustment Agreement was a sham document and the alleged disbursements were in fact fraudulent misappropriations, negating any obligation to reimburse.
Party_Details.Judicial_Reasoning_Log: The court found no evidence supporting the existence of the Entrustment Agreement or the legitimacy of the disbursements.
